senses.definition: The office or function of a procurator; the act of acting as a legal agent or representative on behalf of another person.
examples: The procuracy oversees the prosecution and legal representation in a civil case.
Historical documents refer to the procuracy as the official body responsible for legal affairs in the province.

senses.definition: Obsolete or rare term referring to a procuration or act of appointing a proxy; a formal grant of authority to act on someone’s behalf.
examples: The document granted procural of power to the attorney.
Studying old legal texts, she encountered the word procural as an early form of proxy authority.

senses.definition: Plural form of procural; multiple instances of procural or proxy authority documents.
examples: Several procurals were issued to authorize different representatives.
The archive contains many procurals from the 19th century.

senses.definition: Variant or misspelling of procurance/procuration; generally refers to the act of procuring or procuration as a proxy authority.
examples: The document referred to procurance in older texts.
Historical dictionaries sometimes list procurance as an archaic form of procuration.

senses.definition: Plural of procurance; occurrences of procurance or proxy authority documents in historical contexts.
examples: The library shelves hold several procurances from the medieval period.
Researchers compare procurances across different regions.

senses.definition: To obtain or procure something; to obtain or bring about by effort.
examples: They hope to procure the necessary funding for the project.
The agent managed to procure a rare specimen for research.

senses.definition: The act of procuring or the power to act as an agent; a document granting authority to act for another.
examples: The attorney acted under a procuration from the client.
Procuration is often used in historical contexts to describe a representative's authority.

senses.definition: Plural of procuration; multiple acts or documents granting authority to act as an agent.
examples: The collection includes several procurations from different jurisdictions.
Different procurations were issued to various assistants.

senses.definition: Having the effect of procuring or helping to obtain; acting as a remedy or preventive measure.
examples: The medicine was believed to have procurative properties.
They sought procurative measures to secure funding.

senses.definition: An official who represents someone else in legal matters or manages property or affairs for another person; sometimes used in religious contexts.
examples: The procurator handled the estate for the deceased.
In some systems, a procurator acts similarly to a lawyer or agent.

senses.definition: The office, authority, or jurisdiction of a procurator; the body of prosecutors in some legal systems.
examples: The procuratorate oversees cases and prosecutions in the region.
News reported actions by the procuratorate against corrupt officials.

senses.definition: Relating to procurators or their office; connected with the administration or prosecution of legal matters.
examples: The procuratorial duties include supervising investigations.
A procuratorial tone pervades the official report.

senses.definition: Plural of procuratory; formal documents or statements granting authority to act on someone’s behalf.
examples: The archives contain several procuratories from different eras.
She filed multiple procuratories to authorize assistants.

senses.definition: People who act as agents or representatives for others in legal, financial, or administrative matters.
examples: The procurators manage clients' estates and legal affairs.
Several procurators were appointed to handle different cases.

senses.definition: The position or office of a procurator; the role of managing or representing on behalf of others.
examples: Her procuratorship involved handling numerous financial matters.
The procuratorship was dissolved after reforms.

senses.definition: Plural of procuratorship; multiple offices or roles of procurators.
examples: Several procuratorships were established across provinces.
Historical records show changes in various procuratorships.

senses.definition: Relating to procuration or authority to act as an agent; formal or legal in tone.
examples: A procuratory statement was issued to empower the attorney.
Procuratory language appears in the document.

senses.definition: A female procurator; a woman who acts as an agent or representative for another.
examples: The procuratrix managed the estate on behalf of the heirs.
In historical texts, procuratrix appears as the female counterpart to procurator.

senses.definition: To obtain or get something, often after effort or negotiation.
examples: She managed to procure a tickets to the concert.
They procured the necessary materials for the project.

senses.definition: Past tense of procure; obtained or acquired something after effort.
examples: They procured the necessary equipment.
She procured a permit last week.

senses.definition: The process of obtaining goods or services; the act of purchasing for an organization.
examples: Procurement teams source supplies and manage contracts.
Procurement is a key function in many companies.

senses.definition: Plural of procurement; multiple instances of obtaining goods or services.
examples: The company reviewed several procurements before choosing a supplier.
Public procurements were announced this quarter.

senses.definition: A person who obtains or arranges to obtain something; a finder or supplier.
examples: The company acts as a procurer of rare materials.
She hires a procurer to locate hard-to-find components.

senses.definition: People who obtain or arrange to obtain something; multiple procurers.
examples: Multiple procurers were involved in sourcing materials.
Procurers work with suppliers to meet demand.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of procure; obtains or provides something.
examples: The company procures raw materials from overseas.
She procures tickets when they go on sale.
